summary: Validate options provided via MongoClientOptions
description:
Due to the behavior described in CDRIVER-3723, libmongoc will not always validate options that are set on a {{mongoc_uri_t}} after its initial creation when using that URI to create a client pool.
We should perform validation ourselves in Swift.

summary: Add timestamp test with high-order bit set that's not 2^32-1
description: See DRIVERS-1319 for updated details.

summary: Overload of BSONEncoder.encode breaks type inference
description: In SWIFT-923, an overload of {{encode}} was introduced to allow encoding to a {{BSON}} instead of a {{BSONDocument}}. This breaks existing code that relies on type inference when invoking the old version of the method. We should update this method to have a distinctly different name to avoid this. Such a name could be {{encodeFragment}} to borrow the [{{JSONSerialization}} terminology|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/jsonserialization/readingoptions/1416042-allowfragments], though this is subject to change until the method is exposed as part of the public API.

summary: Encoding top level arrays crashes
description: Need to add some special case logic for mutable arrays on the BSONEncoder to allow encoding top level arrays

summary: DecodingError when encountering dropDatabase event in change stream
description: The current definition of {{ChangeStreamEvent}} has the {{ns}} field being non-optional. However, this field is not present for ["invalidate" events|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/change-events/#invalidate-event], which causes the change stream to return a {{DecodingError}} from {{next}} whenever such an event is encountered.

summary: Manually clean up mongoc_uri_t when ConnectionString options validation fails
description:
Per conversation on this thread:

[https://forums.swift.org/t/deinit-and-throwing-initializers/38871]

It seems like we can't really rely on the conventional wisdom about when {{deinit}} is actually supposed to be called.

This came up running leak checking in the tests; when we throw an error while validating options on a seemingly fully-initialized {{ConnectionString}}, {{denit}} is never called and we leak the {{mongoc_uri_t}}.

It seems like the safest approach is to have a separate cleanup method we call when we throw in {{init}}, and to call that from {{deinit}} if it hasn't been called already. 

We should make sure this issue doesn't affect any of our other wrapped C types.

I've filed a Swift bug about this:
https://bugs.swift.org/browse/SR-13355

summary: Ensure nil is returned from cursor before returning LogicError
Decimal128 parsing does not throw for error inputs
description: Many of the "parse error" cases from the BSON corpus tests do not throw errors when passed to the {{BSONDecimal128}} initializer from String.

summary: Cursor gets leaked in findOne after DecodingError
Rename swift-bson target to SwiftBSON
description: {{swift-bson}} 's current main module is named {{BSON}}, but this conflicts with the exported {{BSON}} enum, leading to issues for users (and the driver) which depend on {{swift-bson}} directly. We should rename this to {{SwiftBSON}} to reduce the chance of such conflicts.

summary: swift-bson is missing Timestamp initializer that takes in Ints
description:
The driver currently includes an initializer for {{BSONTimestamp}} that accepts {{Int}} s, but {{swift-bson}} does not include one. To avoid breaking changes when we switch to using {{swift-bson}} in the driver, we need to include this initializer.

reference: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/blob/master/Sources/MongoSwift/BSON/BSONValue.swift#L1160]

summary: Upgrade to libmongoc 1.17
description: Once libmongoc 1.17 is released we can upgrade our vendored version.

summary: Make ExceededTimeLimit retryable writes error
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    In SERVER-40493 the server team suggests that drivers should consider ExceededTimeLimit, LockTimeout, and ClientDisconnect as retryable writes errors.
    See DRIVERS-651 for updated details.

summary: Reduce default keepalive time to align with Azure defaults
description: See DRIVERS-1228 for updated details.

summary: Do not add the RetryableWriteError label to errors that occur during a write within a transaction (excepting commitTransaction and abortTransaction)
description: The corresponding spec change clarifies the conditions under which the driver should add the RetryableWritesError label to errors within a transaction, and adds some transaction tests to ensure that the driver does not add the label to errors that occur on writes within a transaction.

summary: Only create monitoring events if the user is actually subscribing to them
description: Right now, we always create command monitoring and SDAM monitoring events, copying over data from libmongoc, even if the user has not registered any handlers. It would be more performant to check if any handlers are registered first.

summary: Implement an efficient method for pre-pending _ids in the new BSON library
description:
Currently, the only way for the driver to do this is by creating a new document starting with an {{_id}} and then copying over each element. This is expensive as all of the values get round-tripped through native Swift types.

Given that this method may be called very frequently in applications where our users rely on the driver to add {{_id}}s, we should add a {{withID}} method to the BSON library that re-uses the encoded bytes.

summary: Support parsing $uuid as extended JSON representation for subtype 4 binary
description: See DRIVERS-848 for updated details.

summary: Create EventLoopBoundMongoClient type
description: Add a new type {{EventLoopBoundMongoClient}} and support for initializing one from a {{MongoClient}}. Implement {{listDatabases()}} on this type, and add tests to confirm the correct event loop is used.

summary: Extended JSON parsing performance improvements
description: Implement performance improvements for extended JSON parsing as per the design (WRITING-6861).

summary: Improve performance of BSONDecoder
description: Implement performance improvements for decoding Swift structs from {{BSONDocument}} as per the design (WRITING-6861). The focus of this ticket is getting the {{swift-bson}} decoder to match the performance of the libbson-based one. Future work will address optimizing the decoder further.

summary: Add more spec tests for invalid relaxed UUIDs
description: See DRIVERS-1526 for updated details.

summary: Improve performance of insertion
description: Implement performance improvements for bulk insertion and insertOne as per the design (WRITING-6861).

summary: MongoClient initializer performs blocking DNS lookup
description: The {{MongoClient}} initializer invokes {{mongoc_client_pool_new}}, which in turn invokes {{mongoc_topology_new}}, which invokes {{_mongoc_client_get_rr}}, which performs a blocking DNS lookup. This means the entire event loop is stalled while the DNS lookup is happening, and this could potentially take a while with a slow network or if the configured DNS server is down.

summary: equalsIgnoreKeyOrder respects key order in documents contained in arrays
Use a struct instead of enum for ExtendedJSONEncoder.mode
description: We should use the struct-as-enum workaround for the ExtendedJSONEncoder.Mode enum in the off chance that a new extended JSON format is introduced that we want to support. Additionally, we should probably name the enum {{ExtendedJSONFormat}} or something similar as it is referred to in the specification.

summary: BSONDocument should conform to Content protocol
description: We should make BSONDocument conform to Vapor's Content protocol so that if desired users can e.g. return BSONDocuments from their API routes.

summary: Support truncatedArrays field in ChangeStream update descriptions
Do not repeatedly resume if getMore receives the same error
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    The [change streams spec resumable criteria is broad|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/197f446c82b271c4f42de3e86af8f2246a5d6b2f/source/change-streams/change-streams.rst#resumable-error], considering all server errors resumable except a few exclusions, or errors with a {{NonResumableChangeStreamError}} label.

Drivers resume once, but we incorrectly assume that if we resume due to an error received on a getMore command, and succeed in sending the aggregate during resume, it is worthwhile to attempt the next getMore command. It is possible for getMores to consistently fail, even though aggregate succeeds.

I believe this warrants modifying either the criteria for resume logic (perhaps making it a whitelist instead of a blacklist), or modifying how we repeat resuming. E.g. perhaps we consider an error of the first getMore to be a failure to resume.

Spec commit: [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/f93d78191f3db2898a59013a7ed5650352ef6da8]
    See DRIVERS-907 for updated details.

summary: SwiftBSON 3.0 missing parsing support for Extended JSON v1 (legacy)
description:
In SwiftBSON 3.0.0, Extended JSON v1 (aka legacy Extended JSON) parsing support was accidentally omitted, which could break user applications. We should reintroduce support for parsing this format of extended JSON to match the expected behavior from the libbson-based library.

See here for repro: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/516#issuecomment-783410666]

summary: Dates greater than or equal to Int64.max ms from epoch cause the driver to crash
description:
Internally in swift-bson, we often convert a Swift {{Date}} to an {{Int64}} containing the number of milliseconds since the epoch, as is required by the BSON spec. The {{Date}} type in Swift is backed by a {{Double}} however, which means it can contain values greater than {{Int64.max}}. When such a value (or even {{Int64.max}} itself, for some reason) is used to construct a date that is written to a document, the application crashes.

This has potential security implications for applications which accept date values from user clients, since even valid input which has been parsed to a Swift type can still crash an application.

For example, the following repro crashes:
{code} let doc: BSONDocument = [ "x": .datetime(Date(timeIntervalSince1970: TimeInterval(Int64.max))) ]
{code}

We should include safeguarding in the driver to ensure such crashes don't occur, possibly by truncating invalid dates when converting to BSON.

summary: Update versioned api transaction-handling spec test
Test failure because $listLocalSessions is not supported in API version 1
description: See DRIVERS-1505 for updated details.

summary: Change estimatedDocumentCount() to use the $collStats Agg Stage Instead of Count Command
Define error handling behavior of writeErrors and writeConcernError on Mongos
Drivers should not inspect error messages for "not master" or "node is recovering"
Rotate Atlas Dev testing credentials
Use "hello" command when API Version is declared
Require passing Stable API options to getMore and transaction-continuing commands
Remove slaveOK connection string option from URI parsing
Convert CRUD v2 spec tests to unified test format
Sensitive commands run via runCommand do not have bodies redacted in command monitoring events
description:
*CVE ID:* CVE-2021-32050

*Title:* Some MongoDB Drivers may publish events containing authentication-related data to a command listener configured by an application

{*}Description{*}:

Some MongoDB Drivers may erroneously publish events containing authentication-related data to a command listener configured by an application. The published events may contain security-sensitive data when specific authentication-related commands are executed.

Without due care, an application may inadvertently expose this sensitive information, e.g., by writing it to a log file. This issue only arises if an application enables the command listener feature (this is not enabled by default).

This issue affects the MongoDB C Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7, MongoDB PHP Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.9.2, MongoDB Swift Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.1.1, MongoDB Node.js Driver 3.6 prior to 3.6.10, MongoDB Node.js Driver 4.0 prior to 4.17.0 and MongoDB Node.js Driver 5.0 prior to 5.8.0. This issue also affects users of the MongoDB C++ Driver dependent on the C driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7 (C++ driver prior to 3.7.0).

*CVSS Score:* 4.2

CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N 

*CWE ID:* CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor 

*Exact affected versions:*
MongoDB C Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7

MongoDB C++ Driver 3.0.0 prior to 3.7.0

MongoDB PHP Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.9.2

MongoDB Swift Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.1.1

MongoDB Node.js Driver 3.6 prior to  3.6.10

MongoDB Node.js Driver 4.0 prior to 4.17.0

MongoDB Node.js Driver 5.0 prior to 5.8.0 

*Is fixed version available:*  Yes

*How the issue was discovered:* Internally

summary: Add test for security-sensitive command monitoring event redaction
Use "hello" command for monitoring if supported
Bump maxWireVersion for MongoDB 5.0
Ensure tests on new servers aren't relying on OP_KILL_CURSORS, OP_INSERT, OP_UPDATE, and OP_DELETE
Disable code cov on travis-ci
description: See DRIVERS-1521 for updated details.

summary: Mitigate pain of using field names with dots and dollars
Stable API Connection Examples for Docs
Expose serviceId in command monitoring events
description: Command monitoring events should now have an optional serviceId property which is present when in load balanced mode.
